title: Verb ずじまい
titleEn: Expressing regret for not having done something; not being able to; missing the chance to do it
structure: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずじまい
structureEn: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずじまい
explanation: Dùng khi cuối cùng đã không làm được điều định làm hoặc lẽ ra nên làm, thường kèm cảm giác tiếc nuối. Có thể hiểu là “rốt cuộc không...”. Cấu trúc: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずじまい.
explanationEn: The grammar point ～ずじまい (zu jimai) is used to express regret for not doing something, not being able to do it, or not getting the chance to do it. It highlights an action that ultimately never happened, despite intentions or opportunities
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb ずとも
titleEn: Means Even without doing (X), (Y) can still happen
explanation: Dùng để nói dù không làm X thì Y vẫn xảy ra hoặc vẫn có thể làm được. Sắc thái trang trọng, gần với なくても. Cấu trúc: Verb-negative stem + ずとも.
explanationEn: The ～ずとも grammar point is used to express that, even if something isnt done, the desired result can still be achieved or the situation remains unaffected. It can be translated as even without or even if not
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb ずにはおかない
titleEn: Expresses cannot help but..., will definitely..., or must do something
structure: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずにはおかない
structureEn: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずにはおかない
explanation: Dùng để nói điều gì chắc chắn sẽ xảy ra hoặc chắc chắn khiến ai đó làm gì. Sắc thái mạnh: “không thể không”, “nhất định sẽ”. Cấu trúc: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずにはおかない.
explanationEn: The ずにはおかない grammar point conveys the sentiment of an inevitable action or a strong emotional compulsion. It can mean I can’t let it pass without doing X or I definitely will do X
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb ずにはすまない
titleEn: Cannot do without..., unavoidable, have to
structure: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずにはすまない
structureEn: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずにはすまない
explanation: Dùng để nói không thể kết thúc mà không làm việc đó, thường vì trách nhiệm, đạo lý hoặc hoàn cảnh bắt buộc. Cấu trúc: Verb-ない form (remove ない) + ずにはすまない.
explanationEn: The grammar point ずにはすまない is used when something must be done due to social/moral obligation, necessity, or rules. It implies that there is no way around taking that action
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb そうにない
titleEn: Expresses unlikelihood or that something does not seem likely to happen
explanation: Dùng để nói một việc có vẻ khó xảy ra hoặc không có dấu hiệu sẽ xảy ra. Cảm giác dự đoán dựa trên tình hình hiện tại. Cấu trúc: Verb-ますstem + そうにない.
explanationEn: The そうにない grammar point is typically formed from the verb’s ます-stem plus そうにない, and is used to express that something seems unlikely to happen or that someone seems unlikely to do something
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb そうもない
titleEn: Expresses negative potential; it doesnt seem like..., its not likely
explanation: Dùng để nói dường như không thể hoặc không có khả năng làm được việc gì. Sắc thái phủ định khả năng mạnh hơn そうにない trong nhiều ngữ cảnh. Cấu trúc: Verb-ますstem + そうもない.
explanationEn: ～そうもない is used to express a low possibility or unlikelihood of something happening. It is typically attached to the ます-stem of a verb, similar to そうにない but with an even stronger sense of improbability in many contexts
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb そばから
titleEn: Expresses that as soon as one action finishes, another begins immediately
explanation: Dùng để nói ngay sau khi làm xong một việc thì việc khác xảy ra, thường là việc làm mất tác dụng của hành động trước. Hay mang sắc thái bực bội. Cấu trúc: Verb-ますstem + そばから.
explanationEn: The 〜そばから grammar point is used to indicate that right after a certain action is completed, another action takes place—often suggesting a repetitive or frustrating cycle
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb たが最後
titleEn: Emphasizes once something happens, there is no going back; once, as soon as, etc
structure: Verb-casual, past (た form) + が最後
structureEn: Verb-casual, past (た form) + が最後
explanation: Dùng để nói một khi đã xảy ra hoặc đã làm X thì không thể quay lại, thường dẫn đến hậu quả xấu. Cấu trúc: Verb-casual, past (た form) + が最後.
explanationEn: The 〜たが最後 grammar point indicates that once an action is taken or event occurs, some (often irreversible) consequence follows. In English, it can be translated as once, as soon as, or the moment that
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb たことにしてください
titleEn: Asking someone to pretend or accept that something has already happened or taken a certain state
structure: Verb (past, casual) + ことにしてください
structureEn: Verb (past, casual) + ことにしてください
explanation: Dùng để yêu cầu người khác xem như một việc đã xảy ra hoặc chấp nhận một giả định. Có thể hiểu là “hãy coi như đã...”. Cấu trúc: Verb (past, casual) + ことにしてください.
explanationEn: The grammar point Verb たことにしてください is used to request someone to act as if or pretend that a certain action has already occurred or a certain state exists. It is used in various contexts to express the idea of accepting a hypothetical situation for convenience or to avoid conflicts
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Verb たら Verb たで
titleEn: Conveys that if something happens (or doesn’t happen), there is a particular consequence or situation either way
structure: Verb-た form + ら + (same Verb-た form) + で
structureEn: Verb-た form + ら + (same Verb-た form) + で
explanation: Dùng để nói nếu X xảy ra thì lại có vấn đề hoặc tình huống riêng của X; dù xảy ra hay không cũng có điều phải tính. Cấu trúc: Verb-た form + ら + (same Verb-た form) + で.
explanationEn: The pattern VerbたらVerbたで is used for scenarios where doing something creates a certain issue or reaction, and not doing it also creates a different concern. It often shows that no matter which side of the condition you end up on, there’s some related result or trouble
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)
